title: amphipappus fremontii subsp. spinosus
intro: Amphipappus fremontii subsp. spinosus, also known as prickly Mormon tea, is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its thorny stems and small yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Amphipappus fremontii subsp. spinosus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
